clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Screen for secondary hypertension when clinical suspicion exists to enable targeted therapy
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Adults with hypertension + clinical clues suggesting secondary cause (young age, resistant hypertension, abrupt onset, etc.)
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Detection, diagnosis, and specific targeted therapy for secondary hypertension

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for secondary HTN screening decision
Display possible secondary causes (renal, endocrine, vascular)
Order appropriate diagnostic tests based on suspicion
Initiate specific therapy based on identified cause
Schedule monitoring and treatment response assessment
Log screening and diagnosis to health record

validationRules: Clinical suspicion must be documented if screening is ordered
Screening only recommended when suspicion present

rules: IF hypertension present AND clinical suspicion present → recommend screening
IF hypertension present AND no clinical suspicion → do NOT screen (avoid overtesting)
Identify specific secondary cause based on clinical clues
Order targeted diagnostic tests based on suspected etiology
IF positive → initiate specific targeted therapy
IF negative → continue primary hypertension management

recommendedNextStep.description: Specific diagnostic test based on clinical clues

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: N/A - screening not for acute crisis

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Order targeted diagnostic testing based on specific clinical suspicion
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: If initial screening negative but suspicion persists, consider advanced testing
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag positive findings for specialist referral
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log COR:1 LOE:C-EO recommendation to clinical decision record with rationale
